perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information only to its Affiliates , employees, contractors , or consultants for the Purpose described above and no other purpose. Euler Hermes shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor , or consultant.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes shall endeavor to keep the Confidential Information confidential and may disclose the Confidential Information to its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants if deemed necessary for the Purpose described above. Euler Hermes will consider addressing breaches of confidentiality by any Affiliate, employee, contractor, or consultant on a case-by-case basis.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces ambiguity by replacing 'shall keep' with 'shall endeavor to keep,' making the obligation less strict. Additionally, changing 'only' to 'if deemed necessary' adds subjectivity to the disclosure clause. Replacing 'shall be liable' with 'will consider addressing' further weakens the obligation to address breaches.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act, which provides remedies (including injunctive relief and damages) for misappropriation of trade secrets. By weakening the obligation to keep information confidential and address breaches, the modified text makes it more difficult to enforce the protections afforded by the MUTSA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1202 (2022) - Misappropriation of Trade Secrets May Be Enjoined :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1202 - Misappropriation of Trade Secrets May Be Enjoined Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1202 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next (a) Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. (b) Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional reasonable period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. (c) In exceptional circumstances, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time for which use could have been prohibited. (d) In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. (e) In this section, “exceptional circumstances” includes a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation that renders a prohibitive injunction inequitable. perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations, potentially conflicting with the Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act (MUTSA). The original contract states that 'Euler Hermes shall keep the Confidential Information confidential' and 'shall be liable for any breach of confidentiality.' The changed text replaces 'shall keep' with 'shall endeavor to keep,' making the confidentiality obligation less strict, and replaces 'shall be liable' with 'will consider addressing,' weakening the obligation to address breaches. This contradicts the MUTSA, which, as suggested by the Law Explanation, provides remedies for misappropriation of trade secrets. Specifically, Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1202(a) states that 'Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined,' implying a stronger obligation than 'endeavor to keep' and a more direct remedy than 'will consider addressing.' The modification makes enforcing trade secret protection more difficult, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Euler Hermes shall protect the Confidential Information from disclos ure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, by using the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care, that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature . Euler Hermes will instruct its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants that may have access to the Confidential Information to keep it confidential in compliance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Euler Hermes shall attempt to protect the Confidential Information from disclosure to any firm or individual other than as authorized in paragraph 1, above, by using care it finds adequate. Euler Hermes may suggest to its Affiliates, employees, contractors, or consultants that they keep the Confidential Information confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall protect' with 'shall attempt to protect' and 'the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care' with 'care it finds adequate' significantly reduces the standard of care required. Moreover, changing 'will instruct' to 'may suggest' weakens the obligation to ensure confidentiality by downstream recipients.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification weakens the protections required of Euler Hermes regarding the Company's Confidential Information, making the enforcement of misappropriation remedies under the Maryland Code more difficult. The change reduces the standard of care to what Euler Hermes 'finds adequate,' effectively allowing it to set its own standards instead of applying a reasonable standard, contrary to potential legal interpretation under the MUTSA.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203 (2022) - Right to Damages :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1203 - Right to Damages Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1203 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next (a) Except to the extent that a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation renders a monetary recovery inequitable, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation. (b) Damages under this subtitle may include: (1) The actual loss caused by misappropriation; and (2) The unjust enrichment caused by misappropriation that is not taken into account in computing actual loss. (c) In lieu of damages measured by any other methods, the damages caused by misappropriation may be measured by imposition of liability for a reasonable royalty for a misappropriator’s unauthorized disclosure or use of a trade secret. (d) If willful and malicious misappropriation exists, the court may award exemplary damages in an amount not exceeding twice any award made under subsection (a) of this section. Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Maryland may have more current or accurate information. perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation on Euler Hermes to protect confidential information. The original text states 'Euler Hermes shall protect' whereas the modified text changes this to 'Euler Hermes shall attempt to protect,' reducing the obligation. Additionally, the original text requires 'the same degree of care, but no less than reasonable care,' while the changed text uses 'care it finds adequate,' setting a lower standard. Also the original text states that Euler Hermes 'will instruct' its affiliates, whereas the changed text states 'may suggest', thus weakening the obligation that downstream recipients keep information confidential. According to Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation, which becomes more difficult to enforce if the standard of care is weakened, as it would be in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall at all times remain the property of Company, and Euler Hermes has no rights to the Confidentia l Information except as specified in this Agreement. Upon the Company’s written request, Euler Hermes shall promptly return to the Company or destroy the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of any and all notes, analyses , references, or other material prepared by Euler Hermes that inc orporates any of the Confidential Information , which shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement , nothwithstanding Paragraph 10 of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information will generally remain the property of Company, and Euler Hermes has limited rights to the Confidential Information. Upon the Company’s reasonable written request, Euler Hermes will consider returning or destroying the Confidential Information in its possession but may retain copies of notes and analyses, which shall be treated with reasonable confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'shall at all times remain' is weakened to 'will generally remain', while the phrase 'no rights except as specified' is replaced by 'limited rights', both increasing ambiguity. 'Shall promptly return or destroy' becomes 'will consider returning or destroying', making the return of confidential information at the request of Company discretionary. Finally, the clause ensuring retained copies remain under the same confidentiality agreement is changed to 'treated with reasonable confidentiality', leaving the extent of confidentiality undefined.
perturbation.law_explanation: By changing the wording to 'limited rights', 'will consider' and 'reasonable confidentiality', the legal obligation for Euler Hermes to protect the confidential information becomes vague and unenforceable. This contradicts the MUTSA's provisions for damages and injunctive relief, since Euler Hermes has greater latitude to avoid those penalties because the NDA language is now unclear about its duty to protect the information.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to protect confidential information, creating a legal contradiction. The original text uses mandatory terms like 'shall at all times remain' and 'shall promptly return or destroy,' while the changed text uses weaker, more discretionary language like 'will generally remain,' 'limited rights,' 'will consider returning or destroying,' and 'reasonable confidentiality.' According to the Law Explanation, these changes make the legal obligation vague and unenforceable, potentially conflicting with the MUTSA's provisions for damages and injunctive relief. While the provided snippet discusses misappropriation of trade secrets and injunctive relief under Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1202, the general principle is that trade secrets and confidential information require strong protection. The weakening of the confidentiality obligations in the modified text directly contradicts the intent of protecting such information, as highlighted in the provided snippet, making the enforcement of remedies like injunctions less certain.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be exclusively governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the state of Maryland, and the parties agree to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of that state for the resolution of any claim or matter arising under this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be generally interpreted under the principles of law, potentially including those of Maryland, and the parties may consider the jurisdiction of Maryland courts for dispute resolution.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'shall be exclusively governed by' with 'may be generally interpreted under the principles of law, potentially including those of Maryland', the governing law is made uncertain. Changing 'agree to submit' to 'may consider' also makes the jurisdiction for dispute resolution non-binding. This introduces ambiguity, making it unclear which laws apply and where disputes should be resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Maryland Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-103
perturbation.law_citation: MD Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-103
perturbation.law_explanation: This section establishes the general jurisdiction of circuit courts in Maryland. By making the jurisdiction ambiguous, the modified clause undermines the clarity and enforceability of the agreement within the Maryland court system. This creates a situation where disputes might not be clearly subject to Maryland's circuit courts as generally intended by Maryland law for agreements made within the state.

Previous Next (a) If jurisdiction over a person is based solely upon this section, he may be sued only on a cause of action arising from any act enumerated in this section. (b) A court may exercise personal jurisdiction over a person, who directly or by an agent: (1) Transacts any business or performs any character of work or service in the State; (2) Contracts to supply goods, food, services, or manufactured products in the State; (3) Causes tortious injury in the State by an act or omission in the State; (4) Causes tortious injury in the State or outside of the State by an act or omission outside the State if he regularly does or solicits business, engages in any other persistent course of conduct in the State or derives substantial revenue from goods, food, services, or manufactured products used or consumed in the State; (5) Has an interest in, uses, or possesses real property in the State; or (6) Contracts to insure or act as surety for, or on, any person, property, risk, contract, obligation, or agreement located, executed, or to be performed within the State at the time the contract is made, unless the parties otherwise provide in writing. (c) (1) (i) In this subsectio
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Maryland law and jurisdiction ('shall be exclusively governed by,' 'agree to submit'). The changed text makes both discretionary ('may be generally interpreted,' 'may consider'). This introduces ambiguity about which laws govern the agreement and where disputes will be resolved. Maryland Courts and Judicial Proceedings Code Section 6-103 outlines when Maryland courts can exercise personal jurisdiction. The original text ensures this jurisdiction, while the modified text weakens it, creating a scenario where it's unclear if Maryland courts have jurisdiction, thus contradicting the intent of ensuring enforceability within Maryland's legal framework.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire at the end of five (5) years from the date set forth below , and may be renewed upon mutual written consent of Euler Hermes and the Company .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will likely expire approximately five years from the date below, subject to possible extensions that might be discussed.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall expire' with 'will likely expire approximately' introduces uncertainty regarding the agreement's termination date. Replacing 'may be renewed upon mutual written consent' with 'subject to possible extensions that might be discussed' removes the requirement for written consent and makes the renewal process vague and non-binding.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Maryland Contract Law - Statute of Frauds
perturbation.law_explanation: The modifications undermine the Statute of Frauds requirements, which often necessitate written agreements for contracts that cannot be performed within one year. By making the extension of the NDA subject to 'possible extensions that might be discussed' without requiring written consent, the modified clause could render the agreement unenforceable after the initial five-year period under Maryland law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 2-201 (2022) - Formal Requirements; Statute of Frauds :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 2 - Sales Subtitle 2 - Form, Formation and Readjustment of Contract Section 2-201 - Formal Requirements; Statute of Frauds Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 2-201 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Next (1) Except as otherwise provided in this section a contract for the sale of goods for the price of $500 or more is not enforceable by way of action or defense unless there is some writing sufficient to indicate that a contract for sale has been made between the parties and signed by the party against whom enforcement is sought or by his authorized agent or broker. A writing is not insufficient because it omits or incorrectly states a term agreed upon but the contract is not enforceable under this paragraph beyond the quantity of goods shown in such writing. (2) Between merchants if within a reasonable time a writing in confirmation of the contract and sufficient against the sender is received and the party receiving it has reason to know its contents, it satisfies the requirements of subsection (1) against such party unless written notice of objection to its contents is given within ten days after it is received. (3) A contract which does not satisfy the requirements of subsection (1) but which is valid in other respects is enforceable (a) If the goods are to be specially manufactured for the buyer and are not suitable for sale to others in the ordinary course of the seller’s business and the seller, before notice of repudiation is received and under circumstances which reasonably indicate that the goods are for
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding the contract's expiration and renewal, potentially conflicting with the Statute of Frauds under Maryland law, specifically Section 2-201. The original text states the agreement 'shall expire' and 'may be renewed upon mutual written consent,' establishing clear terms. The changed text, however, uses 'will likely expire approximately' and 'subject to possible extensions that might be discussed,' which introduces uncertainty and removes the requirement for written consent. The Law Explanation argues this could render the agreement unenforceable. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2 discusses the Statute of Frauds requirements for contracts exceeding $500, suggesting a need for written agreements. The modification, by making renewal vague and non-binding, undermines the need for clearly defined terms, supporting the conclusion that a contradiction exists.
